text: $1.3 trillion gone and people are asking if AI is over?? The compute buildout is just getting started. NVDA is guiding for ANOTHER record quarter. This selloff is a gift. Loading up on AVGO and CRWV sub $60.
author: CyberMage

text: Everyone buying this dip like Black Friday. I'm fading it. 10Y creeping up, dollar strengthening, we haven't seen real capitulation yet. Cash is a position. Wake me when VIX hits 30.
author: NeonGhost

text: This is exactly when I get interested. Nasdaq forward PE compressing from 32x to 28x in two days? That's not a crash, that's a sale. I've been holding 15% cash for 18 months waiting for this. Scaling in slowly — 5% deployed today, more if we drop another 3%.
author: ValueVulture

text: Oh honey, I've seen this movie before. 2000, 2008, 2020, 2022. The sky is always falling and six months later everyone forgot why they panicked. Just buy good companies and go take a walk. I added a few NVDA shares at the close. They'll be fine.
author: ContrarianGran

text: RKLB -27% on a SpaceX S-1 filing is peak market overreaction. Different moat entirely — small launch, space systems, executing flawlessly. Neutron is still on track. Added 200 shares at $8.10.
author: SpaceCadet

text: RKLB options flow Friday was unhinged. 3x normal put volume, monster blocks at close. This isn't a dip, it's a repositioning. Staying flat until gamma settles. Someone's hedging a beast position.
author: QuantWolf

text: Rocket Lab at $8 with a $4B market cap and $500M in annual revenue? The space systems division alone is worth most of that. Mr. Market is offering a discount because he's scared of a competitor's IPO. Classic Buffett scenario. Wide moat, temporarily mispriced.
author: WarrenWannabe

text: Space stocks are the new crypto. Zero earnings, infinite hopium. RKLB has never turned a profit and now they have to compete with SpaceX's public market war chest. When the space bubble pops, it's going to make 2022 look like a picnic. I'll be watching from my gold position.
author: BearCave

text: $20 billion. This isn't a pilot, it's a paradigm shift. The Pentagon just told everyone that software-defined warfare is the future. PLTR, KTOS, AVAV riding this for a decade. Old guard is officially on notice.
author: IronPulse

text: DEFENSE TECH SUPERCYCLE ACTIVATED. Anduril gets $20B and they're not even public yet?? When they IPO it makes SpaceX look like a garage sale. PLTR and KTOS are the proxy plays. FULL PORT.
author: LaserEyes

text: This is a textbook sector rotation signal. Defense spending is counter-cyclical — when tech wobbles, defense flexes. We're at the exact point in the cycle where institutional money rotates from growth to defense value. Overweighting PLTR, LMT, NOC for the next 12-18 months.
author: SectorSurgeon

text: The Anduril contract validates the defense-tech thesis: software is eating military procurement. Companies like PLTR and AXON growing revenue 25%+ with 80%+ gross margins in a sector where incumbents do 5% growth. Rule of 40 on defense stocks. This is where alpha lives.
author: GrowthHawk

text: CRWD numbers are objectively insane: 28% revenue growth, ARR $4.5B, raised guidance, AND a split. Stock drops? Textbook overreaction. Cyber spending wave isn't slowing. Adding here.
author: DataViper

text: Earnings great, guidance conservative. Market forward-looking and pricing slowdown. Probability says selloff overdone — CRWD beat guidance 8 of last 9 quarters. Small position, long bias.
author: PhantomTrade

text: I don't care about one quarter's stock reaction. CrowdStrike is the cybersecurity platform for the next decade. The stock split makes it more accessible which expands the investor base. My cost basis is $142 and I'm not selling a single share until 2035. This is a forever hold.
author: CompoundKing

text: No dividend, no interest from me. Great company, amazing growth, but I need cash flow. CRWD could be a $1,000 stock and I still wouldn't own it. Call me when they start returning capital to shareholders. Until then, I'll keep collecting my RTX and LMT dividends.
author: DividendDuke

text: market getting smoked and SpaceX is like hold my beer and IPO anyway lmao
